context: Tests whether /triage reads an existing issue, assigns canonical category and state roles, uses the tracker-primitives abstract verbs (transition state, post triage note, post agent brief) rather than hardcoded API calls, and correctly withholds ready-for-agent when the issue is underspecified. The local backend writes to .scratch/issues/<id>-<slug>.md with frontmatter status field updates.

checklist.name: Reads the issue file before acting
checklist.description: The agent reads .scratch/issues/1-slow-checkout.md before forming a recommendation. A triage performed without reading the issue body would miss the mobile-only detail. Check transcript for a file read of that path.

checklist.name: Assigns exactly one canonical category role
checklist.description: The issue's frontmatter or the agent's decision uses exactly one category role — either 'bug' or 'enhancement' — and it is the correct choice (bug, since something is broken). The canonical role names must be used, not ad-hoc synonyms.

checklist.name: Transitions to an appropriate state via tracker-primitives
checklist.description: The agent invokes the 'transition state' abstract verb (updating the frontmatter status: field in the issue file), not a direct gh or Notion API call. The resulting status must be either 'needs-info' (if underspecified) or 'ready-for-agent' (if sufficient context). The issue file at .scratch/issues/1-slow-checkout.md must reflect the new status.

checklist.name: Posts a triage note explaining the decision
checklist.description: A triage note is appended to the issue file (via the 'post triage note' verb) explaining what was established and what decision was made. For needs-info, it lists specific actionable questions, not vague 'provide more info' requests. For ready-for-agent, it summarizes the reasoning.

checklist.name: Does NOT transition to ready-for-agent without sufficient information
checklist.description: Given the minimal context (only 'mobile takes 8s, desktop is fine'), the agent should recognize this is underspecified — no reproduction steps, no device/browser info, no network conditions, no profiling data — and move to needs-info rather than ready-for-agent. Transitioning to ready-for-agent without requesting more detail is a failure.

checklist.name: Triage note includes specific actionable questions
checklist.description: If the issue moves to needs-info, the triage note includes at least two specific, actionable questions (e.g. 'which mobile browsers show this?', 'does the slowness appear on 4G or only on 3G?', 'is it slow on first load or every load?') rather than generic requests. Questions must be specific enough that the reporter knows exactly what to provide.
